verdict_reasoning: B4GALT1 (beta-1,4-galactosyltransferase 1) is predominantly a Golgi-resident type II transmembrane protein responsible for lactose and glycan synthesis. However, it is well-documented to have a surface-expressed isoform on certain cell types (notably sperm, leukocytes, and some tumor cells), where it functions as a cell-surface receptor/adhesion molecule that binds glycan ligands. This surface pool is a stable TM-anchored form, not merely transient recruitment. Given the tissue/cell-type restriction of surface expression, verdict is contextual.
